title: YWCA Missoula - Institutional Corruption and RICO Enterprise Liability
description: Institutional corruption case documenting YWCA Missoula's role as a RICO enterprise, including governance fraud, conflicts of interest, federal grant fraud, multi-victim patterns of abuse, child endangerment, HIPAA violations, and systematic institutional dysfunction spanning 2018-2025.
plaintiff_name: Elvis Nuno (primary complainant), Jessica Waltz (named victim), Multiple YWCA victims
defendant_name: YWCA Missoula, YWCA Board of Directors, Detective Connie Brueckner (board member), E'Lise Chard (employee)
notes: Enterprise liability case under RICO statutes. Detective Brueckner's undisclosed YWCA board membership created conflict of interest in criminal investigation. Pattern evidence includes 10+ victims documenting systematic institutional failures, October 2021 Meadowlark winter evictions of 4 families with children, HIPAA violations, and federal grant fraud allegations.

narrative_documents.description: Comprehensive YWCA institutional analysis documenting RICO enterprise patterns (30+ pages)
Expanded evidence documentation with victim accounts and institutional failures

evidence_documents.description: Consolidated financial statements documenting board governance and Connie Brueckner's board membership
Pattern evidence from multiple YWCA victims
Formal complaints to YWCA documenting institutional failures
evidence_documents.notes: Elvis Nuno's June 2018 YWCA complaint (protected First Amendment speech)

agencies_notified: HUD Office of Inspector General (federal grant fraud)
Montana POST (Peace Officer Standards and Training)
key_individuals: Detective Connie Brueckner (YWCA board member, investigator in Nuno case - conflict of interest)
E'Lise Chard (YWCA employee, perpetrator in related case)

named: Elvis Nuno (wrongful prosecution after YWCA complaint)
unnamed: 3 additional families evicted from Meadowlark (October 2021, winter evictions with children)
Multiple YWCA complainants (Reddit, Google Reviews, Facebook)

damages.notes: Includes Elvis Nuno's damages from wrongful prosecution following YWCA complaint, Jessica Waltz and 3 families' eviction damages, and institutional harm to community

regulatory_violations: Montana POST ethical standards violations (Detective Brueckner)

key_evidence_highlights: Detective Connie Brueckner served on YWCA board while investigating Elvis Nuno (undisclosed conflict)
Elvis Nuno filed YWCA complaint June 2018 (protected speech), arrested August 2018
Fraudulent search warrant August 2018 (Brueckner did not disclose YWCA conflict)
October 2021 Meadowlark evictions: 4 families with children evicted in winter
Jessica Waltz (named victim) documented eviction trauma
YWCA financial statements 2023-2024 confirm Brueckner as board member
10+ victim accounts via Google Reviews, Reddit, Facebook (pattern evidence)
Arthur Brown Google Review documents YWCA institutional dysfunction
HIPAA violations documented in Facebook threads
Federal grant fraud allegations (HUD funding misappropriation)

rico_enterprise_elements.enterprise_liability_theory: YWCA Missoula operated as RICO enterprise through systematic pattern of fraud, conflicts of interest, and retaliation against complainants

victims.name: Jessica Waltz
victims.harm: Winter eviction with children, housing instability, trauma

legal_issues: YWCA mission violation (organization purportedly serves women and families)

arweave_archival.notes: All documents public record. Institutional corruption documentation critical for public accountability.
